title: Queenstown vs Wanaka for Families — Which Should You Choose?
meta_desc: Queenstown vs Wanaka with kids — honest 2026 comparison of costs, activities, and family vibe. Which NZ destination is better for your family? ✓
intro: Queenstown and Wanaka are two of New Zealand's most popular destinations, but which is better suited for families? With similar landscapes and outdoor activities, the key differences lie in their atmosphere and costs. For a family of four, a week's accommodation in Queenstown can cost upwards of $2,500, whereas Wanaka may offer more affordable options from $1,800.

sections.heading: Activities for All Ages
sections.body: Both Queenstown and Wanaka offer a wide range of activities for families, including skiing, hiking, and water sports. However, Queenstown's Skyline Gondola and Luge ($40 - $60 per person) and Wanaka's Puzzling World ($20 - $30 per person) are popular attractions that cater to different age groups.

sections.heading: Vibe and Atmosphere
list: Queenstown: bustling, vibrant, and lively, with a wide range of restaurants and bars
Wanaka: more relaxed, peaceful, and family-friendly, with a strong sense of community

sections.heading: Driving Between Queenstown and Wanaka
sections.body: The drive between Queenstown and Wanaka takes approximately 1 hour, depending on traffic and road conditions. Families can consider renting a campervan or car from companies like Jucy or Apex, with prices starting from $40 - $100 per day.

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body: For families looking for more affordable accommodation options, consider visiting our campervans page for a range of vehicles and prices, starting from $80 - $200 per night.
